purpose: Route requests to the narrowest shipped top-level Labs21 template skill while refusing to claim moved or deleted families that no longer belong to the suite router.
selection_order: Continuity and reflective control beat artifact creation because they decide whether work should continue, compact, or be challenged before more output is produced.
Prompt-artifact work beats skill and family routing when the deliverable itself is the prompt.
Skill-package authoring beats domain-family routing when the requested artifact is a reusable skill package.
Stage-gated product definition beats delivery workflows when the request is still idea -> blueprint -> requirements -> system design.
Reusable workflow frameworks beat delivery-control and design-token routing when the user wants a Superpowers-replacing harness pack rather than a single feature lane.
Design-token specification beats delivery-control and generic reasoning when the deliverable is a design-token spec rather than implementation or analysis.
Software-delivery routing beats generic reasoning when the task is really about non-trivial feature lifecycle choices rather than broad analysis.
Direct startup viability teardown beats generic reasoning because harsh commercial survivability is a narrower job than analytical advice.
If the request falls outside the shipped suite boundary, do not force a Labs21 route.

children.name: context-compaction
children.summary: Creates a compact continuation snapshot for long sessions, handoffs, or context-window pressure.
route_when: The user wants a handoff, continuation summary, compacted session state, or context reduction.
The main need is preserving session truth across compaction or session switches rather than doing the underlying task.
avoid_when: The user wants a new plan, implementation change, or domain analysis rather than a continuation artifact.
The request is a confidence check or retrospective without any need to compact context.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this top-level skill before routing when the runtime does not already have the Labs21 continuation helper available.

children.name: self-cognitive
children.summary: Runs confidence checks, retrospectives, and repeatable-workflow extraction when the main need is explicit verification or learning.
route_when: The user wants a confidence check, preflight verification, postmortem, retrospective, or reusable workflow extraction.
The main need is calibrated judgment under risk rather than building the underlying artifact.
avoid_when: The user still needs the core work performed rather than reviewed or extracted into lessons.
The request is really about delivery-control stage selection or prompt design rather than reflective verification.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this top-level skill before routing when the runtime does not already have the Labs21 verification helper available.

children.name: meta-prompting
children.summary: Designs or repairs prompt artifacts such as system prompts, prompt templates, rubrics, and prompt architectures.
route_when: The requested deliverable is the prompt artifact itself.
The user needs prompt architecture, system-prompt structure, rubric design, or prompt evaluation guidance.
avoid_when: The user already has a sparse generation prompt and only needs it enriched, not architected.
The request is about creating a reusable skill package rather than a prompt artifact.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this top-level skill before routing when the runtime does not already have the Labs21 prompt-architecture helper available.

children.name: prompt-augmentation
children.summary: Expands a sparse text, image, or video generation prompt without redesigning the surrounding prompt architecture.
route_when: The user already has the subject and wants richer wording, clearer direction, or a few useful prompt variants.
The request is specifically about augmenting a generation prompt rather than redesigning the whole prompt system.
avoid_when: The user wants a system prompt, prompt template, rubric, or prompt architecture artifact.
The request is about skill packaging, project planning, or software delivery rather than prompt wording.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this top-level skill before routing when the runtime does not already have the Labs21 prompt-enrichment helper available.

children.name: create-skill
children.summary: Creates or upgrades a reusable leaf skill package, including portable guidance and packaging structure.
route_when: The user wants a reusable skill package, skill scaffold, or portable leaf-skill rewrite.
The package's main job is one repeatable workflow rather than family routing.
avoid_when: The package's main job is routing among child skills with explicit metadata.
The request is about using or editing a prompt artifact rather than authoring a reusable skill package.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this top-level skill before routing when the runtime does not already have the Labs21 leaf-skill authoring helper available.

children.name: create-router-skill
children.summary: Creates or upgrades a router package whose main job is selecting among child skills with explicit metadata and honest fallbacks.
route_when: The user wants to create or upgrade a router skill, family entrypoint, or child-inventory package.
The package needs explicit child metadata, selection rules, install hints, or truthful fallback behavior.
avoid_when: The package is a single leaf workflow and does not need family routing.
The request is ordinary prompt design or domain analysis rather than reusable router-package authoring.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this top-level skill before routing when the runtime does not already have the Labs21 router-authoring helper available.

children.name: labs21-product-suite
children.summary: Routes raw ideas, blueprints, PRDs, and system design through the staged product-definition suite before defined-feature delivery starts.
route_when: The request is still raw product ideation, MVP framing, PRD writing, or baseline system design rather than defined-feature delivery.

The team needs the staged product-definition path that produces durable `docs/reference/design.md`, `docs/reference/requirements.md`, and `docs/reference/implementation.md` outputs before coding workflow begins.
avoid_when: The product direction is already defined and the main need is proposal -> contract -> execute -> evaluate -> compound delivery.
The request is startup survivability pressure-testing rather than product-definition work.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this top-level family router before routing when the runtime does not already have the Labs21 product-definition suite available.

children.name: harness-engineering
children.summary: Provides a front-door Anthropic-harness workflow pack for proposal -> contract -> execute -> evaluate -> compound without replacing the specialist delivery lanes.
route_when: The user wants a complete coding workflow framework or a single front door for proposal -> contract -> execute -> evaluate -> compound.
The main need is an Anthropic-harness-style workflow pack rather than a single delivery-control lane.
avoid_when: The user only needs a specialist delivery-control lane such as harness design, contract approval, browser QA, or postflight extraction; route those to delivery-control instead.
The request is raw product ideation, PRDs, or system architecture from scratch; use labs21-product-suite first.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this top-level leaf skill before routing when the runtime does not already have the harness-engineering framework available.

children.name: generating-design-tokens
children.summary: Generates design-token specifications from brand guidelines, mockups, or design-system requirements.
route_when: The user wants a design-token specification, token JSON, or token-layer artifact derived from brand or design-system input.
The main deliverable is the token spec itself rather than broader design-family routing or ordinary implementation.
avoid_when: The request is about broad design-family routing across foundations, generative UI, or liquid-glass experimentation rather than a token spec.
The task is plain analysis, software delivery, or startup viability rather than design-token generation.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this top-level skill before routing when the runtime does not already have the Labs21 design-token generation helper available.

children.name: delivery-control
children.summary: Routes defined software-delivery work to the right delivery lane across harness design, contract approval, frontend evaluation, and compounding.
route_when: The request is about delivering a non-trivial software feature and the next specialist delivery lane is not obvious yet.
The team needs harness design, contract approval on a proposed implementation slice, strict frontend acceptance, or post-delivery compound extraction before work should continue.
avoid_when: The request is a simple repo edit or debugging task that now falls outside the shipped Labs21 suite boundary.
The hard problem is design-family routing, startup survivability, or analytical reasoning rather than delivery-stage selection.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this top-level family router before routing when the runtime does not already have the Labs21 delivery family available.

children.name: startup-pressure-test
children.summary: Runs a harsh startup viability teardown using realistic CAC, retention, monetization, burn, and runway pressure.
route_when: The user wants an investor-style critique, launch stress test, or commercial survivability verdict for a startup or business model.
The main need is a cold-eyed economic teardown rather than software delivery, design routing, or generic reasoning.
avoid_when: The request is about prompt artifacts, skill packages, design-family choice, or software delivery rather than commercial viability.
The user needs broad strategic reasoning without startup-survival math or pressure testing.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this top-level skill before routing when the runtime does not already have the Labs21 startup-teardown helper available.

children.name: using-reasoning
children.summary: Routes analytical, strategic, or diagnostic requests to the right reasoning child when more than one reasoning lane could fit.
route_when: The request is mainly analytical, strategic, or diagnostic and the right reasoning lane is not obvious yet.
The user could plausibly need state calibration, problem framing, scenario implications, hidden-rule survivability checks, advisory analysis, or multi-lens problem solving.
avoid_when: The request is really startup viability, software delivery, or design-family routing rather than general reasoning-family selection.
The user already knows the exact reasoning child they need and does not need the family router to narrow it.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this top-level family router before routing when the runtime does not already have the Labs21 reasoning family available.
